LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PRESIDIO ADVISORY SERVICES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT AND JOINDER IN KPMG, LLP'S DEMURRER. THE COURT DISCLOSED THAT A CURRENT EMPLOYEE (ALI ABTAHI) OF PLTF'S COUNSEL'S FIRM PREVIOUSLY WORKED FOR THE LAW & MOTION DEPT. AS A LEGAL RESEARCH ASSISTANT. THE COURT DID NOT RECUSE ITSELF. DEFENDANT DAVID RIVKIN'S COUNSEL, PAUL J. PFINGST, MADE AN ORAL MOTION CHALLENGING JUDGE QUIDACHAY PURSUANT TO CCP 170.6. THE COURT GRANTED THE MOTION & TRANSFERRED THE MATTER TO THE PRESIDING JUDGE'S DEPT. 206 FOR RE-ASSIGNMENT. (PURSUANT TO DEPT. 206, THIS MATTER IS NOW RE-ASSIGNED TO JUDGE BUSCH IN DEPT. 210.) DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-27-2005 Continued To May-27-05 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 210
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT KPMG LLP'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCE: Nancy L. Fineman, Esq. & David A. Carducci, Esq. of Cotchett, Pitre, Simon & McCarthy 650/697-6000 for pltf; J. Warren Rissier, Esq. of Gibson, Dunn & Crutcher LLP 213/229-7409 for deft KPMG LLP; Ellen K. Brown, Esq. of Latham & Watkins LLP 391-0600 for deft Presidio Advisory Services, LLC; Michael Patrick McNamara, Esq. of McNamara, Spira & Smith 310/979-2584 for deft Gregg Ritchie; Paul J. Pfingst, Esq. of Higgs, Fletcher & Mack LLP 619/236-1551 for deft David Rivkin.
RULING: The Court disclosed that a current employee (Ali Abtahi) of pltf's counsel's firm previously worked for the law & motion dept. as a legal research assistant. The Court did not recuse itself. Defendant David Rivkin's counsel, Paul J. Pfingst, made an oral motion challenging Judge Quidachay pursuant to CCP 170.6. The Court granted the motion & transferred the matter to the presiding judge's dept. 206 for re-assignment. (Pursuant to Dept. 206, this matter is now re-assigned to Judge Busch in Dept. 210.) (302/REQ/JT) Court Reporter Beldon Yee, CSR #2647
